references.text: [Sh76] Shader, L., All right triangles are Ramsey in $\mathbbE^2$!. J. Comb. Th. A (1976), 385-389.

remarks.text: For some colourings a single equilateral triangle has to be excluded, considering the colouring by alternating strips. Shader \cite{Sh76} has proved this is true if we just consider a single right-angled triangle.

statement.text: In any $2$-colouring of $\mathbb{R}^2$, for all but at most one triangle $T$, there is a monochromatic congruent copy of $T$.
